summary: Stress testing the three A5.1 scenarios reveals that the base case is most fragile to macro shocks (±74 pp Sharpe swing under wage inflation), while the bull case exhibits moderate sensitivity to Fed policy (±24 pp). The bear case remains resilient to incremental credit spread moves (±8 pp) because it already prices severe execution failures.

rows.label: Bull Case +1σ: Fed holds rates at 3.64% (no cuts)

extras.description: Fed does not cut rates as forecast; cost of capital rises 50 bp. Fair value compresses 7% due to higher discount rate. Expected return falls from +33.1% to +23.9%, Sharpe ratio from 65.8% to 45.3%. Triggers edge toward base case execution risk.
extras.key_risk: Capital raise terms become less favorable; Stellantis partnership terms worsen

rows.label: Bull Case -1σ: Fed cuts 100 bp to 2.64%

extras.description: Fed cuts aggressively beyond consensus 50 bp; cost of capital falls 100 bp. Fair value expands 8% due to lower discount rate and non-dilutive capital access. Expected return rises from +33.1% to +43.8%, Sharpe ratio from 65.8% to 89.6%. Reinforces bull case capital optionality.
extras.key_upside: Non-dilutive Stellantis financing confirmed; production ramp low-risk; first-mover regulatory advantage vs. Joby validated

rows.label: Base Case +1σ: Wage growth rises to 4.0% YoY

extras.description: Aerospace wage inflation accelerates; production ramp constrained to 120 aircraft vs. base 150. Revenue falls to $600M, EV/Revenue multiple compresses to 2.0x (execution doubt). Stock price collapses 65% from base case to $1.13 (-82.6% from current $6.48). Cascades into bear case capital crisis dynamics.
extras.key_downside: Aerospace sector 56% hiring difficulty + 29% workforce retirement wave + tight labor market (4.2% unemployment) create wage inflation spiral. Production bottleneck becomes binding constraint. Forced additional dilutive capital raise.
extras.cascade_risk: Leads to bear case outcomes if combined with any Type Cert or Abu Dhabi delays

rows.label: Base Case -1σ: Wage growth falls to 2.35% YoY

extras.description: Labor cost inflation eases; production ramp improves to 180 aircraft vs. base 150. Revenue rises to $900M, EV/Revenue multiple expands to 3.0x (execution confidence improves). Stock price gains 50% from base case to $4.88 (+24.7% from current). Approaches bull case territory if other drivers also execute.
extras.key_upside: Fed rate cuts improve labor supply; wage growth moderates from tight 3.4% to 2.35%. Archer achieves production ramp without premium labor costs. Dilution reduced; net cash position improves. Sets stage for upward revaluation if Abu Dhabi and Type Cert confirm on schedule.
extras.optionality: Achieves base case revenue targets at lower cost; opens path to bull case if capital remains non-dilutive

rows.label: Bear Case +1σ: HY spreads widen to 400 bp

extras.description: Credit market shock; HY spreads widen from forecast 300-350 bp to 400 bp. Capital raise forced at distressed valuation (30% discount). Equity dilution increases; stock price falls 13% from bear base of $1.18 to $1.03. Expected return worsens from -81.8% to -84.1%, Sharpe ratio from -189.3% to -193.6%.
extras.key_risk: Incremental downside limited in bear case because equity already deeply distressed. Additional spread widening primarily affects cost of debt/convertible raise; equity holder already pricing severe execution failures.
extras.contagion: Coupled with +1σ wage inflation shock, this creates compounding liquidity crisis requiring restructuring

rows.label: Bear Case -1σ: HY spreads tighten to 250 bp

extras.description: Credit market improvement; HY spreads tighten from forecast 300-350 bp to 250 bp. Capital can be raised on non-dilutive debt terms (convertible, secured financing). Equity dilution avoided; stock price improves 20% from bear base of $1.18 to $1.42. Expected return improves from -81.8% to -78.1%, Sharpe ratio from -189.3% to -181.3%.
extras.key_upside: Even with favorable credit conditions, bear case remains deeply negative; requires recovery in execution drivers (Abu Dhabi launch on schedule, FAA Type Cert delivered, production ramp to 150+ aircraft) to exit downside scenario.
extras.optionality: Favorable spreads buy time for execution turnaround; eliminates worst-case 'dilution death spiral' but does not reset cash runway if revenue misses persist
